commit.message:
GA Development: Skill Preserving Mode (SPM) (#18313)

Transitions the experimental Skill Preserving Mode (SPM) to General
Availability (GA) with production-ready code, documentation, and tests.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task
[963617590095831371](https://jules.google.com/task/963617590095831371)
started by @BrianCLong*

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Updated Python runtime version in CI/CD pipelines.

* **New Features**
* Enhanced container security with hardened, multi-stage builds and
non-root execution.
* Added runtime security monitoring and threat detection for containers.
  * Implemented automated container vulnerability scanning and analysis.
* Introduced comprehensive security policy enforcement framework
including network policies and access controls.
* Added Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) generation for supply chain
transparency.

(Underdog), Close Combat + Circle Throw (Black Belt), Comeback Kid +
Dodge Dip Duck Dive and Dodge (Athlete), Venoshock (Ninja), Beauty's
New Allies + Ravishing Style (Beauty Modeler), Cute's Allies + Cute's
New Allies + Dainty (Cute Idol), Mental Potential + Smart's Allies +
Smart's New Allies (Smart Teacher), Immovable + Tough's Allies +
Tough's New Allies (Tough Guy), Cardinal Concert + Carrying Chorus +
Heroic Hymn (Bard), Buffering + Supreme Presence + Supreme
Intimidation (Conduit), Substitute (Guardian), Survival of the Fittest
(Shaman), Reflect Type + Teleport + Trick Room (Psychic), Fake Tears +
Featherdance + Hex (Hex Maniac), More Than Ever (Coach), Summoning Sign
(Signer).

commit.message:
fix: remove expired placeholder dates from seed rules + add date extraction to scraper

Seed JSON rules had hardcoded validUntil: "2025-12-31" causing all seeded
rules to be filtered out by the matching engine since Jan 2026. Removed
fake dates (null = always valid) and updated scraper pipeline to extract
real validity dates from source websites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Hyperliquid skill packages + comprehensive API reference docs

Three new specialized skills for Hyperliquid agents:
- hl-data-fetcher: Info REST + WebSocket, paginated fills, rate-limit patterns
- hl-trading: Exchange endpoint, EIP-712 signing, all order types + TWAP/transfers
- hl-analytics: Trader PnL/Sharpe/drawdown, market snapshot, funding screener

Also adds docs/hyperliquid-api-reference.md — master API reference combining
official docs and source code patterns from this codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: stop passing slug when creating clerk organizations (#3696)

Remove the `slug` parameter from all `createOrganization` calls to the
Clerk SDK. Clerk will auto-generate its own slug, which we never read
anyway — only `clerkOrg.id` is used. This eliminates slug conflicts
(HTTP 422) and slug drift between local DB and Clerk.

Closes #3692

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update Calendly link to personal booking URL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(backend): Resolve F26 debt — DEBT-076 through DEBT-080

DEBT-077 (P1): POST /interest-runs no longer requires account ID query params.
  Account IDs auto-resolved from chart_of_accounts by standard codes.
  run_by extracted from JWT instead of query param.

DEBT-078 (P1): POST /interest-waivers same fix — approved_by from JWT,
  account IDs auto-resolved.

DEBT-079 (P1): Added POST /interest-runs/{run_id}/reverse endpoint.
  Creates reversal events and journal entries for each charge, marks
  run as reversed. Uses existing InterestRunRepository.mark_reversed().

DEBT-076 (P2): POST /levy-runs/{run_id}/reverse — removed user_id query
  param, now extracted from JWT.

DEBT-080 (P2): Added GET /schemes/{id}/interest-waivers with pagination.
  Uses existing InterestWaiverRepository.list_by_scheme().

Tests: 2775 passed, 43 skipped, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: configure repo for mcp_coder usage (#56)

* feat: configure for mcp_coder usage

Configure repository for AI-assisted development with mcp_coder:

MCP Server Configuration:
- Add .mcp.windows.json for code-checker and filesystem servers
- Configure reference projects (mcp_coder, mcp-code-checker)

Claude Code Setup:
- Add .claude/CLAUDE.md with mandatory development instructions
- Add .claude/settings.local.json with pre-approved permissions
- Create slash commands: /commit_push, /implementation_review, /plan_review, /rebase
- Add claude_local.bat/sh launchers with environment setup

GitHub Actions:
- Update CI workflow with parallel checks (black, isort, pylint, pytest, mypy)
- Add label-new-issues.yml for automatic issue labeling
- Add approve-command.yml for /approve workflow progression
- Add dependabot.yml for weekly dependency updates

Development Tools:
- Add format_all, reinstall, pylint_check, mypy_check, pytest_check scripts
- Support both Windows (.bat) and Unix (.sh) platforms

Configuration:
- Add .gitattributes for line ending management
- Update .gitignore for MCP configs and VSCode Claude status
- Add MCP_CODER_SETUP.md comprehensive setup guide

Code Quality:
- Apply isort formatting to main.py (import order)

* feat: add architecture enforcement tools

Add comprehensive architecture enforcement to maintain code quality
and prevent common architectural issues.

Architecture Tools:
- Add import-linter for contract-based import validation
- Add tach for module boundary enforcement
- Add pycycle for circular dependency detection
- Add vulture for dead code detection

Configuration Files:
- .importlinter: Define layered architecture and library isolation
- tach.toml: Define architectural layers and module dependencies
- vulture_whitelist.py: Whitelist MCP handlers and pytest fixtures
- .large-files-allowlist: Track files allowed to exceed size limits

Tool Scripts (Windows & Unix):
- tools/lint_imports: Check import contracts
- tools/tach_check: Verify architectural boundaries
- tools/pycycle_check: Detect circular dependencies
- tools/vulture_check: Find unused code
- tools/run_all_checks: Run all quality + architecture checks

CI/CD Integration:
- Update CI workflow with architecture job (PR-only)
- Runs import-linter, tach, pycycle, vulture on pull requests
- Parallel execution with fail-fast disabled

Dependencies:
- Add architecture optional dependency group in pyproject.toml
- Add pytest-xdist and mypy to dev dependencies

Documentation:
- Add docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with architecture guide
- Update MCP_CODER_SETUP.md with architecture tools section
- Document layer responsibilities and enforcement

Claude Code:
- Update settings.local.json with architecture tool permissions
- Allow running architecture checks via Bash tool

* chore: add claude launchers and align gitignore with mcp_coder

- Add claude.bat and claude_local.bat for launching Claude Code with MCP env vars
- Rename .mcp.windows.json to .mcp.json so it is tracked and auto-loaded
- Update .gitignore to match mcp_coder golden template (platform-specific .mcp.*.json ignored, .mcp.json tracked)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(claude): align .claude config with mcp_coder reference

- Use format_all.sh instead of raw black/isort commands
- Add file size check section to CLAUDE.md
- Add issue_analyse and plan_approve commands
- Update rebase to use dynamic base branch via mcp-coder
- Update implementation_review to use mcp-coder tools
- Fix .mcp.json reference project paths (GitHub -> VSCC)
- Remove claude_local.bat/sh (wrong import check for this repo)
- Update settings.local.json permissions

* chore(claude): add missing workflow commands and align permissions

Add 10 missing .claude/commands/ files from mcp_coder reference:
check_branch_status, discuss, implementation_approve,
implementation_finalise, implementation_needs_rework,
implementation_new_tasks, issue_approve, issue_create,
issue_update, plan_update.

Update settings.local.json: add all corresponding Skill permissions,
remove direct-command Bash entries (lint-imports, tach check, etc.)
in favour of ./tools/ script versions only.

* chore: fix .mcp.json portable path and add optional dev tools

Fix .mcp.json: replace hard-coded C:\Users\Marcus path with
${USERPROFILE} env var for portability.

Add optional development tools from mcp_coder reference:
- tools/pydeps_graph.sh/.bat: dependency graph via pydeps
- tools/tach_docs.sh/.bat/.py: architecture HTML docs via tach
- tools/get_pytest_performance_stats.bat: test duration stats
- tools/test_profiler.bat/.generate_only.bat: cProfile integration
- tools/test_profiler_plugin/: pytest plugin + report generator
- tools/__init__.py: package marker for plugin import

* chore: align tools and settings with reference repo

- Remove broken docs/architecture/dependency_graph.html permission
- Rename mypy_check.bat to mypy.bat

* chore: remove pr_info directory from repo

* fix(tests): isolate tests using tmp_path to fix parallel race conditions

- Change project_dir fixture to use pytest tmp_path for per-test isolation
- Simplify setup_and_cleanup fixture (tmp_path handles cleanup)
- Remove stale test artifact files from tests/testdata/tests/

Fixes 3 intermittent failures in parallel test runs (-n auto):
- test_append_file (shared TEST_FILE written by concurrent tests)
- test_edit_file_indentation (temp file deleted by parallel cleanup)

* fix: downgrade pycycle constraint to >=0.0.8 (0.0.9 not on PyPI)

* chore: align pyproject.toml with reference project

- Move [build-system] to top, add wheel dependency
- Add pytest addopts: --strict-markers, --tb=short, -n auto, -p no:tach
- Add pylint messages_control (disable W1203/W0621, enable R0401)
- Add mypy overrides for pytest.* and tests.*
- Update tach>=0.6.0 and vulture>=2.14 to match reference

* feat(ci): add file-size check using mcp-coder

* fix: resolve vulture and import-linter CI failures

- Remove unused edits_applied variable from edit_file.py
- Add pytest autouse fixtures to vulture whitelist
- Remove stale main->mcp import ignore from .importlinter

* chore(ci): temporarily disable import-linter (see issue #57)

* chore(review): delete MCP_CODER_SETUP.md, improve reinstall.bat

- Delete MCP_CODER_SETUP.md (contained stale references)
- Update tools/reinstall.bat to match reference project pattern:
  checks for uv, guards against no-venv, installs .[dev],
  verifies import and CLI entry point

* chore(tools): collapse redundant install steps in reinstall.bat

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: mcp_server_filesystem
repository.description: MCP File System Server: A secure Model Context Protocol server that provides file operations for AI assistants. Enables Claude and other assistants to safely read, write, and list files in a designated project directory with robust path validation and security controls.

Switch matching to per-year comparison (Year1↔Year1, Year2↔Year2, Year3↔Year3)

Previously the 3 seasons were averaged into a single vector before comparison,
which could mask year-to-year shape differences. Now each year is compared
independently and the distances are combined with YEAR_WEIGHTS [0.25, 0.35, 0.40].
level_norm is retained for the similarity reason text only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add cross-lecture guardrails to prevent out-of-scope answers

Add "covered_elsewhere" classification to the RAG pipeline that detects
when a question matches OTHER lectures but NOT the current one. This
prevents Peter Pandey from acting as a general tutor using cross-lecture
content (e.g., answering CREATE VIEW questions while on HAVING Clause).

RAG pipeline changes:
- New CURRENT_LECTURE_MIN_SCORE threshold (0.30) for leak detection
- Cross-lecture ratio check in _classify_results()
- covered_elsewhere handling in both streaming and non-streaming paths
- Redirect message naming the specific lecture that covers the topic

System prompt changes:
- LECTURE SCOPE BOUNDARY rule: don't answer from general knowledge
- Tightened SCOPE section: practical application limited to current lecture
- NEVER DO: explicitly block cross-lecture content teaching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: arena PvP system + immersive RPG scenario messages

ARENA (!arene / !combat):
- Streamer !arene [max] opens PvP arena with inscription phase (60s)
- Viewers type !combat to enter (first come first served)
- Entry fee: 20% of gold goes to bank pot
- Tournament bracket: pairs fight, losers eliminated (+10% gold to pot)
- Champion takes ENTIRE bank pot + XP bonus
- Immersive scenario messages (rotating castle/coliseum stories)

IMMERSIVE MESSAGES:
- Boss waves: "Les murs tremblent!", "Vous êtes le DERNIER REMPART!"
- Countdown: sentinels, trembling earth, cracking gates
- Mini-boss: scouts, forest shadows, gate breaches
- Gold/loot monsters: treasure guardians, mad merchants, stolen gold
- Duels: crowd reactions, blood and glory narratives
- All messages rotate randomly for variety

- Streamer panel button + !arene command + help updated
- Sky stays transparent, everything else opaque

feat(agentic): add predictive orchestration selector, hybrid context manager, and Agentic RAG scaffolding (#18311)

### Motivation

- Provide an explicit, testable decision surface for choosing
orchestration style (single-agent / centralized / hybrid) to reduce
error amplification and improve runtime performance tradeoffs.
- Reduce LLM context cost and leakage by introducing a hybrid
observation-masking + summarization manager that yields token/cost
metrics.
- Scaffold an Agentic RAG layer for IntelGraph (memorizer + researcher +
graph expander) with intent compilation and evidence budgeting so
retrieval+reasoning can be integrated safely and deterministically.

### Description

- Added `src/orchestration/architectureSelector.ts` implementing
`ArchitectureSelector` with `predictOptimalArchitecture` and
`monitorErrorAmplification` and wired it into orchestration planning in
`src/agents/index.ts` via a simple `deriveTaskSignalsFromAnalysis`
heuristic.
- Implemented hybrid context management in
`src/context/hybridContextManager.ts` and a lightweight LLM adapter
`src/context/llmAdapters/simpleLLMClient.ts` providing masking, periodic
summarization, and token-estimate metrics.
- Added Agentic RAG scaffolding in `src/intelgraph/agenticRag.ts`
including `MemorizerAgent`, `ResearcherAgent`, `GraphExpander`, an
`IntentCompiler` abstraction, evidence budgeting, and `AgenticRAG`
facade for `retrieve` and `multiStepReasoning` flows.
- Tests and integration metadata: added unit tests under `tests/` for
architecture selector, hybrid context manager, and Agentic RAG; added
documentation `docs/agent-orchestration-and-agentic-rag.md`; registered
prompt and task spec entries in `prompts/registry.yaml` and
`agents/examples/`; and recorded the decision in
`packages/decision-ledger/decision_ledger.json` and updated
`docs/roadmap/STATUS.json`.

### Testing

- Added unit tests: `tests/orchestration/architectureSelector.test.ts`,
`tests/context/hybridContextManager.test.ts`, and
`tests/intelgraph/agenticRag.test.ts` (tests authored but not executed
in this rollout).
- Ran repository parallelization/boundary check with `node
scripts/check-boundaries.cjs`, which completed and returned no boundary
violations.
- No full unit/integration test suite was executed here; please run
`pnpm test`, `pnpm --filter intelgraph-server test:unit`, or the CI
pipeline to validate the new tests and ensure coverage thresholds are
met.

Add Chinese (zh-Hans/zh-Hant), Singlish locales & Chinese font (#102)

## Summary

- Split `zh` locale into `zh-Hans` (Simplified) and `zh-Hant`
(Traditional Chinese) with script-based codes
- Add `en-SG` (Singlish) locale with light-touch flavor on casual UI
chrome, formal content stays standard English
- Dynamically load Noto Sans SC/TC via Google Fonts per Chinese locale
(no unnecessary downloads)
- Wire up `useTranslation()` in 10 components that had hardcoded English
strings (ErrorBoundary, ShareAcceptPage, LoginButton, SettingsDialog,
HubPage, CollaborationPresence, PrintAnnotations, QuickEmojiPicker,
ReplyInput, ReplyThread)
- Add `fallbackLng` object for browser `zh-CN`/`zh-TW`/`zh-HK` detection
chains
- Set `html[lang]` on language change for screen reader support
- Migrate legacy `"zh"` localStorage selection to `"zh-Hans"`
- Fill all missing translation keys across zh-Hans to match en
- Add CJK font-family CSS overrides via `data-cjk-font` attribute

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run build` — zero errors
- [x] `bun run lint` — zero errors
- [x] `bunx prettier --check .` — passes
- [x] `bun run test:run` — 155 files, 1857 tests passed
- [x] `bun run test:e2e` — 39 tests passed
- [ ] Visual: switch to zh-Hans, zh-Hant, en-SG in settings and verify
text + font rendering

feat(PX-735): complete recording consent framework implementation

- Add S3 recording cleanup service with purge functionality
- Create /api/cron/purge-recordings endpoint for daily retention cleanup
- Add vercel.json with cron schedule (2 AM daily)
- Enhance consent.ts with audit logging for recordings marked for deletion
- Add inbound call handling to voice webhook (US-5: always prompt consent)
- Integrate UnrecordedCallModal into call-interface (US-4)
- Expand E2E tests with webhook simulation and API tests

Implementation covers:
- S3 key extraction from various URL formats
- Idempotent S3 deletion with audit trail
- 30-day retention enforcement via cron
- Inbound call consent flow (always prompts regardless of prior status)
- Post-call documentation modal for unrecorded calls

commit.message:
feat(runtime): implement signed checkpoint lineage — Task 13 (#18)

Add cryptographic agent identity (Ed25519 keypairs) and signed checkpoint
hash chains, enabling verifiable checkpoint authenticity and divergent
lineage detection without trusting the executing node.

New packages:
- pkg/identity/: agent Ed25519 keypair generation, serialization, persistence
- pkg/lineage/: checkpoint signing, verification, content hashing (CID-compatible)

Checkpoint format v0x04 (209-byte header) extends v0x03 with:
- 32-byte PrevHash (SHA-256 chain to previous checkpoint)
- 32-byte AgentPubKey (Ed25519 public key)
- 64-byte Signature (covers header + state, excluding signature slot)

Key changes:
- Refactor ParseCheckpointHeader from 8 return values to CheckpointHeader struct
- Extend storage Provider with SaveIdentity/LoadIdentity/DeleteIdentity
- Migration protocol carries agent identity in AgentPackage
- Inspector displays lineage fields and verifies signatures
- CLI generates/loads agent identity on startup

Derives from: EI-3 (Checkpoint Lineage Integrity), EI-11 (Divergent Lineage
Detection), OA-1 (Canonical Logical Identity)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix earnings estimator hrs/week input losing focus on typing (#41)

The hrs/week and weeks/year inputs were calling renderEarningsEstimator()
on every keystroke, which runs a full recalculation loop over all shifts.
This is unnecessary since changing hrs/week doesn't affect avg pts/hr.
Changed both inputs to call updateEEResult() instead, which just
recomputes the annual estimate from the current input values.

Bumps SW cache to v78.

Co-authored-by: Riley Crandall <rileycrandall04@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
